tree.name: Jonathan Kuminga
tree.narrative: **Jonathan Kuminga** — the former #7 pick with explosive athleticism and star upside — came to Atlanta alongside **Buddy Hield** when the Hawks flipped **Kristaps Porzingis** to Golden State in February 2026. Kuminga's chain traces back through a web of moves: Porzingis came from Boston in a three-team deal, which required assets from the **De'Andre Hunter** and **Bogdan Bogdanović** trades. The deepest root? **Jeff Teague**, drafted by Atlanta in 2009.
tree.tradeDescription: Hawks trade Porzingis to Warriors for Kuminga and Buddy Hield
tree.note: Former #7 pick (2021), explosive athletic wing with star potential

assetsGivenUp.name: Kristaps Porzingis
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Kristaps Porzingis** — the 2024 NBA champion with Boston — came to Atlanta in a July 2025 three-team blockbuster. The Hawks sent **Georges Niang** and a 2031 second to the Celtics, plus **Terance Mann** and **Drake Powell** to Brooklyn, to land the 7'2" unicorn. He lasted just seven months before being shipped to Golden State for Kuminga and Hield.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: Hawks got Porzingis from Celtics, sent Niang + 2031 CLE 2nd to BOS, Mann + Drake Powell to BKN

assetsGivenUp.name: Georges Niang
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Georges Niang** — 'The Minivan' — came to Atlanta in the February 2025 **De'Andre Hunter** trade with Cleveland. The stretch four was then rerouted to Boston just five months later as part of the **Porzingis** three-team deal.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks traded De'Andre Hunter to Cavaliers for Niang and Caris LeVert. Cleveland also received a trade exception.
assetsGivenUp.note: The Minivan - stretch four. Sent to BOS in Porzingis deal.

assetsGivenUp.name: De'Andre Hunter
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **De'Andre Hunter** was the #4 overall pick in 2019, acquired by Atlanta in a draft-night megadeal with New Orleans. The Hawks paid picks #8, #17, #35, and two Cleveland seconds for the Virginia product. The #4 pick itself originated with the Lakers via the Anthony Davis trade. Hunter spent six years in Atlanta before being dealt to Cleveland for **Niang** and **Caris LeVert**.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks traded picks #8, #17, #35, CLE 2021 2nd, and CLE 2022 2nd to Pelicans for #4 pick (used on Hunter), Solomon Hill, #57, and a 2023 2nd. Pick originally from LAL via Anthony Davis trade.
assetsGivenUp.note: Virginia product, #4 overall. Pick originated with Lakers, went to Pelicans in AD trade, then to Atlanta in draft-night deal.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2019 1st Round Pick (#8)
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own #8 pick in 2019, sent to New Orleans as part of the package for the #4 pick used on **De'Andre Hunter**.
assetsGivenUp.note: Atlanta's own pick, sent to NOP in Hunter draft-night trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2019 1st Round Pick (#17) via BKN
assetsGivenUp.narrative: This Brooklyn first-rounder came to Atlanta in the **Taurean Prince** trade, then was immediately sent to New Orleans in the **De'Andre Hunter** draft-night deal. The Pelicans used it on **Nickeil Alexander-Walker**.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks traded Taurean Prince and a 2021 2nd-round pick to Nets for Allen Crabbe, the #17 pick, and a protected 2020 1st-round pick.
assetsGivenUp.note: Originally Brooklyn's own 1st-round pick. Sent to NOP in Hunter draft-night trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: Taurean Prince
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Taurean Prince** came to Atlanta in a 2016 three-team draft-night deal — the Hawks sent **Jeff Teague** to Indiana, and Utah sent the #12 pick (Prince) to Atlanta. Prince was later traded to Brooklyn in 2019, netting the #17 pick that fed into the Hunter trade.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: 3-team trade: ATL sent Jeff Teague to IND, IND sent George Hill to UTA, UTA sent #12 pick (Prince) to ATL.
assetsGivenUp.note: Baylor product, drafted 12th by Utah. ATL acquired via 3-team deal on draft night. Sent to BKN in 2019 for #17 pick.

assetsGivenUp.name: Jeff Teague
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Jeff Teague** — Atlanta's own #19 pick in 2009 and a 3x All-Star with the Hawks. Sent to Indiana in a 2016 three-team deal that brought **Taurean Prince** to Atlanta.
assetsGivenUp.note: Hawks' own pick, 19th overall in 2009. 3x All-Star with ATL. Sent to IND in 3-team deal that brought Taurean Prince.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2021 2nd Round Pick (#49)
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own 2021 second-round pick, sent to Brooklyn in the **Taurean Prince** trade.
assetsGivenUp.note: Atlanta's own 2021 2nd-round pick (#49). Sent to Brooklyn as part of the Taurean Prince trade. BKN used it to draft Zegarowski (Creighton).

assetsGivenUp.name: 2019 2nd Round Pick (#35)
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own 2019 second-round pick, included in the New Orleans package for the #4 pick (**De'Andre Hunter**).
assetsGivenUp.note: Atlanta's own pick, sent to NOP in Hunter draft-night trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2021 2nd Round Pick (#35) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.narrative: A Cleveland second-rounder from the 2017 Kyle Korver trade, sent to New Orleans in the **De'Andre Hunter** deal. NOP used it on **Herbert Jones**.
assetsGivenUp.note: CLE's 2021 2nd, acquired by ATL in Korver trade (2017), sent to NOP in Hunter draft-night trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2022 2nd Round Pick (#47) via CLE
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Another Cleveland second from the Korver trade, rerouted to New Orleans in the Hunter deal. Eventually used by Memphis to draft **Vince Williams Jr.**
assetsGivenUp.note: CLE's 2022 2nd (#47), acquired by ATL in Korver trade (2017), sent to NOP in Hunter draft-night trade. NOP re-routed to MEM, who drafted Vince Williams Jr.

assetsGivenUp.name: Terance Mann
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Terance Mann** came to Atlanta from the Clippers in February 2025 when the Hawks sent **Bogdan Bogdanović** and a Minnesota second to L.A. The versatile wing was then rerouted to Brooklyn five months later in the **Porzingis** three-team deal.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Hawks traded Bogdan Bogdanović and a 2025 2nd round draft pick (MIN own, became Mohamed Diawara) to Clippers for Mann and Bones Hyland
assetsGivenUp.note: Versatile wing. Sent to BKN in Porzingis deal.

assetsGivenUp.name: Bogdan Bogdanović
assetsGivenUp.narrative: **Bogdan Bogdanović** signed with Atlanta via sign-and-trade from Sacramento in November 2020 on a 4-year, $72M deal. Shipped to the Clippers in February 2025 for **Terance Mann**.
assetsGivenUp.note: Signed via sign-and-trade from Sacramento Kings

assetsGivenUp.name: 2025 2nd Round Pick (#31) via MIN
assetsGivenUp.narrative: A Minnesota second-rounder acquired from Houston in a five-team trade in July 2023. Sent to the Clippers with **Bogdanović** for **Terance Mann**.
assetsGivenUp.tradeDescription: Acquired from Houston in 5-team trade involving Dillon Brooks
assetsGivenUp.note: Minnesota's 2025 2nd-round pick, acquired by ATL via HOU in 5-team trade July 2023. Sent to LAC in Mann trade.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2025 1st Round Pick (#22)
assetsGivenUp.narrative: Atlanta's own 2025 first-round pick, used to draft **Drake Powell** at #22 and immediately sent to Brooklyn in the **Porzingis** three-team trade.
assetsGivenUp.note: Hawks' own 2025 first-round pick. ATL drafted Drake Powell #22, then traded him to Brooklyn in Porzingis 3-team deal.

assetsGivenUp.name: 2031 2nd Round Pick via CLE
assetsGivenUp.narrative: A distant Cleveland 2031 second-rounder, sent to Boston as a sweetener in the **Porzingis** three-team deal.
assetsGivenUp.note: Cleveland's 2031 2nd-round pick, sent to Boston in Porzingis 3-team deal.
